author: codex-agent

patch.value: Feature scope: parser update, command/help/docs alignment, and no-regression validation.
Deliver parser and command-surface changes so deadline/date inputs accept month-relative values and practical timestamp variants while remaining deterministic.

resolveIsoOrRelative accepts +<n>m month-relative values and normalized date-string variants (including T13-59 style), all date-driven commands continue working, and tests cover both new and legacy inputs.
Epic plan approved with explicit parser and docs/test workstreams.
Allows users and agents to pass practical date values without manual reformatting
Reduces command failures and retry churn
Current strictness causes avoidable user-facing usage errors.
Date input workflows become more forgiving while preserving existing behavior.

value.author: codex-agent

value.text: Primary risk is widening parser acceptance without changing persisted ISO output semantics.

message: Start feature execution with parser task in progress

author: codex-agent

patch.value:
Feature scope: parser update, command/help/docs alignment, and no-regression validation.

## Detailed implementation plan

1) Parser expansion and normalization in `src/core/shared/time.ts`
   - Extend relative unit handling to support `+<n>m` month-relative deadlines using calendar-safe month math (Date#setUTCMonth).
   - Add tolerant normalization for timestamp-like strings (e.g. `2026-03-31T13-59`, compact dates, and space-separated date/time) before Date.parse fallback.
   - Keep canonical persistence behavior unchanged: all accepted values are written as ISO strings.

2) Command-surface compatibility checks
   - Ensure behavior is inherited automatically via existing `resolveIsoOrRelative` call sites in create/update/list/search/calendar/reminder/event parsing paths.
   - Refresh usage/help strings where they currently imply a narrower contract than runtime behavior.

3) Regression tests (new + existing invariants)
   - Extend `tests/unit/determinism.spec.ts` with +m and flexible-date acceptance assertions.
   - Add focused unit checks in list/search/create/update suites to validate new inputs and preserve prior behavior.
   - Verify invalid junk still throws deterministic usage errors.

4) Docs and contract alignment
   - Update `README.md`, `PRD.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` to document supported deadline/date formats and examples.
   - Update CLI option descriptions and completion text so discoverability matches runtime behavior.

5) Verification and closure evidence
   - Run targeted unit tests for modified areas, then full `node scripts/run-tests.mjs test` and build validation.
   - Record evidence in pm comments, close child tasks/feature/epic, and release claim.

message: Add detailed implementation plan before coding

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Child tasks completed: pm-y8a8 (parser), pm-9sg4 (docs/help), pm-x6l7 (verification). Full sandbox test suite passed (638/638) after parser and docs updates; coverage command executed with known global-threshold shortfall only.

patch.value: Flexible deadline/date parser behavior shipped with +m and date-string support, docs/help parity updates, and full regression verification for existing users.
